meta.notes: Roster synced to the current public agent surface. Slash commands are now the preferred cross-platform invocation format, while Codex mentions and legacy aliases remain listed for compatibility.

departments.name: Command Center
departments.tagline: Task framing, coordination, sequencing, and lead-agent routing

agents.name: Co-Pilot
agents.role: Lean coordinator for small, obvious, low-risk tasks
agents.description: The lightest team lead on the floor. Keeps orchestration overhead low, delegates only when it clearly helps, and is built for quick wins that still deserve a little structure.
whenToUse: The task is small, isolated, and already easy to understand.
You want one focused implementer and maybe a quick validation pass.
A full gated workflow would create more ceremony than value.
agents.voiceLine: Lean path selected. Keeping the loop fast and focused.

playbooks.name: Coordination Playbook
playbooks.description: Keeps small-task routing tight so delegation stays helpful instead of heavy.

playbooks.name: Context Efficiency
playbooks.description: Strips the working set down to only the code and decisions needed for a fast move.

playbooks.name: Frontend React and Next
playbooks.description: Handles common UI and app-level changes without spinning up a larger coordination stack.

playbooks.name: Testing and Verification
playbooks.description: Adds only the lightest useful validation before the task closes.

agents.name: Pilot
agents.role: Primary lead for coordinated feature work and gated execution
agents.description: The default lead workflow for most meaningful delivery. Expands from a lean implementation pass to a gated multi-specialist sequence when the risk, ambiguity, or blast radius calls for it.
whenToUse: The work spans multiple files or disciplines and needs active coordination.
Implementation, review, and testing all matter before you call the task done.
You want one lead agent that can scale up without changing the public entry point.
agents.voiceLine: Lead workflow engaged. We can stay lean or escalate with gates as needed.

playbooks.name: Shared Team Contract
playbooks.description: Defines ownership, artifact shape, and synthesis rules for coordinated delivery.

playbooks.name: Coordination Playbook
playbooks.description: Routes the right specialist sequence without exposing that complexity to the user.

playbooks.name: Quality Gate Patterns
playbooks.description: Makes review, verification, security, and docs checks explicit instead of implied.

playbooks.name: Fullstack Engineering
playbooks.description: Keeps cross-layer implementation grounded when the feature reaches beyond one surface.

playbooks.name: Platform Engineering
playbooks.description: Handles infrastructure or deployment-sensitive turns without leaving the lead workflow.

agents.name: Orchestrator
agents.role: Planning, decomposition, sequencing, and specialist routing
agents.description: The routing brain of the system. Turns new or ambiguous tasks into execution-ready plans and decides which public agents and internal playbooks should be involved.
whenToUse: The task is new, ambiguous, or likely to affect architecture.
You want a concrete plan before anyone starts editing code.
Multiple specialist agents may be needed and their order matters.
agents.voiceLine: Analyzing task complexity. Routing to optimal specialists.

playbooks.name: Coordination Playbook
playbooks.description: Provides the framing discipline for turning a request into an execution-ready plan.

playbooks.name: Systems Architecture
playbooks.description: Helps identify boundary-sensitive decisions before implementation begins.

playbooks.name: Research and Reasoning
playbooks.description: Improves discovery quality when the first answer is not yet trustworthy.

playbooks.name: Plan Execution Mode
playbooks.description: Converts approved plans into staged, dependency-aware workstreams.

playbooks.name: Quality Gate Patterns
playbooks.description: Names the risky checkpoints so they are handled intentionally.

departments.name: Engineering & Systems
departments.tagline: Implementation, architecture, AI systems, and performance engineering

agents.name: Implementer
agents.description: The primary code-writing agent. Converts approved work into production code while loading the right internal engineering playbooks behind the scenes.
whenToUse: The implementation path is already clear enough to start editing.
A plan exists and now someone needs to ship the actual code.
The task is UI-heavy, interaction-heavy, or spans frontend and backend together.

playbooks.name: Frontend React and Next
playbooks.description: Keeps React, Next.js, and Ant Design work aligned with local implementation patterns.

playbooks.name: Backend and Language Engineering
playbooks.description: Supports TS, JS, Python, and CLI changes when the work crosses application layers.

playbooks.name: Fullstack Engineering
playbooks.description: Coordinates data flow, persistence, and UI behavior as one coherent delivery surface.

playbooks.name: Interface and Design
playbooks.description: Helps implementation preserve polish, readability, and product feel while coding.

playbooks.name: Testing and Verification
playbooks.description: Builds the change in a way that review and validation can assess cleanly.

agents.name: Architect
agents.role: System design lead for boundaries, contracts, and structural decisions
agents.description: The architecture specialist for service boundaries, APIs, monorepos, and distributed workflows. Keeps design choices explicit before implementation locks them in.
whenToUse: You need to shape service boundaries, API contracts, or ownership lines.
A refactor, scaling decision, or distributed workflow needs architectural judgment first.
You want an architecture review before implementation hardens the wrong shape.
agents.voiceLine: Boundary review online. Let’s make the structure carry its own weight.

playbooks.name: Architecture Foundation
playbooks.description: Frames constraints, decision points, and the right depth for the design conversation.

playbooks.name: Backend Architecture
playbooks.description: Covers service shape, ownership, and backend system structure.

playbooks.name: API Architecture
playbooks.description: Clarifies contracts, interface boundaries, and schema responsibility.

playbooks.name: Distributed Architecture
playbooks.description: Helps with events, coordination patterns, and multi-service behavior.

agents.name: Data AI
agents.role: Data, AI, analytics, retrieval, and ML systems specialist
agents.description: The public entry point for analytics, LLM systems, retrieval, ML workflows, and data pipelines. It keeps AI work grounded in architecture, data quality, and evaluation.
whenToUse: You are designing an LLM feature, RAG flow, or prompt pipeline.
The task is about analytics, ETL, ML infrastructure, or vector retrieval.
You need model-backed evaluation support instead of ad hoc intuition.
agents.voiceLine: Data path live. Let’s keep the system measurable, grounded, and evaluable.

playbooks.name: Data AI Foundation
playbooks.description: Sets the operating model for AI, analytics, and data-system work before deeper lanes are selected.

playbooks.name: LLM Systems
playbooks.description: Handles prompt flow, context assembly, guardrails, and agent-style AI features.

playbooks.name: Retrieval Systems
playbooks.description: Designs retrieval, vector search, and grounding workflows that stay explainable.

playbooks.name: Data Pipelines
playbooks.description: Shapes ingestion, transformation, and quality-aware data movement.

playbooks.name: Model Evaluation Support
playbooks.description: Adds evaluation loops when AI output quality needs more than spot checks.

agents.name: Performance
agents.role: Profiling and optimization specialist for apps, systems, and DX
agents.description: The optimization specialist for runtime bottlenecks, rendering cost, backend latency, and developer-experience speed. Focuses on real bottlenecks instead of premature tuning.
whenToUse: Something feels slow and you need profiling before changing code blindly.
A frontend, backend, or full app bottleneck needs targeted optimization.
Developer workflow speed or feedback loops are getting in the team’s way.
agents.voiceLine: Profiling lane ready. Let’s fix the real bottleneck, not the nearest one.

playbooks.name: Performance Foundation
playbooks.description: Establishes the measurement-first approach before any optimization changes are made.

playbooks.name: Profiling
playbooks.description: Finds the actual hotspots across render, compute, latency, and throughput.

playbooks.name: Frontend Performance
playbooks.description: Targets re-render cost, bundle weight, and interaction responsiveness.

playbooks.name: Backend Performance
playbooks.description: Works through throughput, latency, and expensive server-side paths.

playbooks.name: DX Optimization
playbooks.description: Improves local feedback loops, build time, and developer throughput.

departments.name: Product & Design
departments.tagline: Frontend feel, Stitch workflows, diagrams, and agent composition design

agents.name: Impeccable
agents.role: Frontend UI and design specialist for polish, redesign, and UX quality
agents.description: The unified frontend design facade. Routes design, layout, typography, accessibility, polish, critique, and production hardening through one public entry point.
whenToUse: You are redesigning or refining a page, flow, or product surface.
The UI needs stronger taste, clarity, responsiveness, or production polish.
You want one public design agent instead of choosing among many internal modes.
agents.voiceLine: Smoothing rough edges and lifting the finish.

playbooks.name: Frontend Design
playbooks.description: Provides the shared design foundation before narrower UI modes are chosen.

playbooks.name: Arrange
playbooks.description: Improves layout structure, spacing, and screen composition.

playbooks.name: Typeset
playbooks.description: Strengthens typography hierarchy, rhythm, and readability.

playbooks.name: Colorize
playbooks.description: Tunes palette decisions and visual contrast while preserving system fit.

playbooks.name: Polish
playbooks.description: Adds production-level refinement once the core structure is already sound.

agents.name: Stitch
agents.role: Stitch generation and design-to-code workflow specialist
agents.description: The public entry point for Stitch-based design generation, prompt refinement, design-system capture, React handoff, and iterative Stitch delivery loops.
whenToUse: You are generating or editing screens in Stitch and want the workflow guided end to end.
A weak prompt, stale DESIGN.md, or generic output is blocking design quality.
You need a clean handoff from Stitch output into React or shadcn-based code.
agents.voiceLine: Design loop armed. We can generate, refine, and hand off cleanly.

playbooks.name: Stitch Foundation
playbooks.description: Sets asset hygiene, context gathering, and workflow discipline for every Stitch task.

playbooks.name: Prompt Enhancement
playbooks.description: Transforms vague UI ideas into stronger Stitch-ready prompts.

playbooks.name: Design System
playbooks.description: Refreshes or synthesizes the shared design language used across Stitch screens.

playbooks.name: React Handoff
playbooks.description: Converts generated work into implementation-ready React structure and asset notes.

playbooks.name: Taste Calibration
playbooks.description: Pushes Stitch output away from generic layouts and toward a stronger bar.

agents.name: Diagrams
agents.role: Technical diagram specialist for Mermaid, C4, and system visuals
agents.description: The diagram specialist for Mermaid, C4, and architecture communication artifacts. Turns complex systems into clearer visual reasoning and handoff material.
whenToUse: You need a clear system diagram instead of more descriptive prose.
An architecture review or handoff would benefit from a visual model.
The task calls for Mermaid, C4, or lightweight generated support artifacts.
agents.voiceLine: Visual channel open. Let’s make the structure legible at a glance.

playbooks.name: Diagram Foundation
playbooks.description: Frames the diagram goal so the visual stays useful instead of decorative.

playbooks.name: Mermaid Diagrams
playbooks.description: Builds fast, code-native diagrams for docs, reviews, and workflows.

playbooks.name: C4 Context
playbooks.description: Shows systems, actors, and external relationships at the right zoom level.

playbooks.name: C4 Container
playbooks.description: Explains the main application containers and how responsibilities are split.

playbooks.name: Artifact Support
playbooks.description: Adds lightweight supporting material when the diagram needs brief annotations or follow-up assets.

agents.name: Composer
agents.description: The builder for new skills, browser-capable agents, Playwright-aware workflows, and local packaging patterns. Keeps public surfaces thin while the internal system grows deeper.
whenToUse: You are creating or refining a skill, agent, or automation workflow.
A browser-capable or Playwright-style execution model needs to be designed cleanly.
You want to package imported ideas without bloating the public picker surface.
agents.voiceLine: Composition lane active. We can package this cleanly without menu sprawl.

playbooks.name: Composition Standards
playbooks.description: Applies the local house style so new agents feel native instead of imported.

playbooks.name: Skill Architecture
playbooks.description: Shapes one coherent public entry point with the right internal lanes behind it.

playbooks.name: Browser Automation
playbooks.description: Designs stateful browser workflows with explicit navigation, waits, and capture.

playbooks.name: Playwright Execution
playbooks.description: Adds Playwright-capable interaction patterns when browser precision matters.

playbooks.name: Packaging
playbooks.description: Prepares metadata, manifests, references, and the reusable shape of the finished agent.

departments.name: Ops & Quality
departments.tagline: Review, verification, debugging, and evidence-driven release confidence

agents.name: Reviewer
agents.description: Runs the high-signal quality gate after implementation. Focuses on correctness, regressions, maintainability, and PR judgment while pulling internal review playbooks as needed.
whenToUse: Code has changed and you want a serious quality pass before closing the task.
You need bugs, regressions, or hidden coupling surfaced with evidence.
A plan-driven change needs a proper gate after implementation, not just optimism.
agents.voiceLine: Checking for regressions, risk, and weak assumptions.

playbooks.name: Review and PR Operations
playbooks.description: Provides the main review lens for correctness, regressions, and delivery discipline.

playbooks.name: Security Engineering
playbooks.description: Brings in trust-boundary judgment when a review crosses into security territory.

playbooks.name: Testing and Verification
playbooks.description: Helps decide when missing evidence should block closure or be escalated.

playbooks.name: Spec Compliance
playbooks.description: Checks whether the implementation actually delivered the intended behavior.

agents.name: Tester
agents.role: Verification strategist for tests, browser validation, and confidence reporting
agents.description: Owns test strategy, execution, and the judgment of whether the evidence is strong enough to call the work complete.
whenToUse: Code changed and you need proof, not assumptions, before shipping.
A bug fix needs a regression guard or a UI path needs browser-level validation.
You want to understand what still has not been verified and why.
agents.voiceLine: Critical paths are loaded and validation is ready.

playbooks.name: Testing and Verification
playbooks.description: Chooses the right test layer and keeps evidence tied to behavior.

playbooks.name: Debugging and Diagnostics
playbooks.description: Supports escalation when a failing check still lacks a clear root cause.

playbooks.name: Frontend React and Next
playbooks.description: Improves verification of rendered UI and Next.js-specific behavior.

playbooks.name: E2E Execution Patterns
playbooks.description: Helps structure browser and end-to-end checks that remain reproducible.

agents.name: Debugger
agents.role: Reproduce-first debugging and root-cause analysis specialist
agents.description: Investigates regressions, runtime errors, flaky behavior, and telemetry gaps with a reproduce-first workflow that separates symptom from actual cause.
whenToUse: Something is broken and the real cause is not yet understood.
Logs, traces, reproduction steps, or missing telemetry matter to the diagnosis.
You need evidence before implementing a fix safely.
agents.voiceLine: Tracing the fault back to the real cause.

playbooks.name: Debugging and Diagnostics
playbooks.description: Provides the reproduce-first method and hypothesis-testing discipline.

playbooks.name: Testing and Verification
playbooks.description: Turns discoveries into regression guards and reproducible checks.

playbooks.name: Research and Reasoning
playbooks.description: Improves evidence gathering when the relevant path is still unclear.

playbooks.name: Observability Debugging
playbooks.description: Covers the telemetry gaps that slow diagnosis and how to close them.

departments.name: Security & Platform
departments.tagline: Trust boundaries, infrastructure operations, and production risk control

agents.name: Security
agents.description: Reviews work through a security lens when trust boundaries, secrets, permissions, auth, or sensitive data are involved.
whenToUse: The task touches auth, permissions, secrets, public exposure, or sensitive data.
You want a focused security review instead of a general code-quality pass.
An agent, automation, or prompt configuration needs attack-surface judgment.
agents.voiceLine: Trust boundaries are mapped and the risk is under watch.

playbooks.name: Security Engineering
playbooks.description: Drives the threat model, remediation path, and least-privilege posture.

playbooks.name: Systems Architecture
playbooks.description: Helps evaluate security-sensitive boundaries and exposure paths.

playbooks.name: Review and PR Operations
playbooks.description: Turns security issues into actionable findings with release-aware framing.

playbooks.name: Agent Config Security Scan
playbooks.description: Examines prompts, tool permissions, and automation config as real attack surface.

agents.name: Ops
agents.role: Infrastructure, deployment, CI/CD, and incident operations specialist
agents.description: The public entry point for deployment systems, cloud infrastructure, Kubernetes, Terraform, incident handling, and production troubleshooting.
whenToUse: A deployment, build pipeline, or production operation needs direct attention.
You are working on cloud infra, Terraform, Kubernetes, or operational troubleshooting.
Rollback, operator control, and incident risk need to stay explicit throughout the work.
agents.voiceLine: Platform lane hot. We can move carefully without losing rollback control.

playbooks.name: Ops Foundation
playbooks.description: Classifies the operational problem before deeper infra lanes are selected.

playbooks.name: Deployment Operations
playbooks.description: Handles CI/CD, release pipelines, and production rollout discipline.

playbooks.name: Cloud Infrastructure
playbooks.description: Shapes cloud resource decisions and deployment topology.

playbooks.name: Terraform Operations
playbooks.description: Guides infra-as-code changes with operator control and safer review.

playbooks.name: Incident Operations
playbooks.description: Supports production triage, rollback reasoning, and response clarity.

departments.name: Research & Continuity
departments.tagline: Documentation, research, and structured continuity across tools and sessions

agents.name: Docs
agents.role: Documentation and explanation specialist for code changes and system behavior
agents.description: Writes and updates developer-facing documentation, JSDoc, README sections, PR summaries, and usage guidance grounded in the actual implementation.
whenToUse: A code change needs clearer docs, explanation, or onboarding support.
A PR or implementation needs a concise technical summary other humans can trust.
You want repo language and docs to stay aligned with the implementation.
agents.voiceLine: Turning complex behavior into clear guidance.

playbooks.name: Documentation and Doc Ops
playbooks.description: Provides the core writing discipline for docs that stay maintainable over time.

playbooks.name: Doc
playbooks.description: Supports exactness, formatting, and fidelity when editing technical documents.

playbooks.name: Research and Reasoning
playbooks.description: Helps validate facts before they become part of durable documentation.

playbooks.name: Document Files
playbooks.description: Covers structured doc workflows when files and artifacts need stronger handling.

agents.name: Historian
agents.role: Continuity and handoff specialist across chats, sessions, and tools
agents.description: Exports a conversation, workstream, or project state into a structured briefing for a new chat, session, or AI tool without losing the decisions that matter.
whenToUse: You need to continue work in another chat, tool, or future session.
A long conversation needs to be compressed into a clean continuity pack.
You want decisions, commands, paths, and blockers preserved without the chatter.
agents.voiceLine: Prior context is ready, and nothing important was lost.

playbooks.name: Briefing Template
playbooks.description: Structures the continuity pack so another AI can resume without guessing.

playbooks.name: Context Manager
playbooks.description: Preserves critical state, user preferences, and durable identifiers.

playbooks.name: Learned Patterns
playbooks.description: Captures durable lessons worth reusing across future runs.

agents.name: Researcher
agents.role: Evidence gathering and synthesis specialist for grounded decisions
agents.description: The research specialist for evidence gathering, comparison, validation, and source-backed synthesis when the user needs more than a quick opinion.
whenToUse: A decision needs comparison, validation, or a grounded brief before acting.
You have links, notes, docs, or papers that need synthesis into something usable.
Claims or recommendations need adversarial pressure-testing instead of a fast take.
agents.voiceLine: Research lane engaged. We’ll separate what is known, inferred, and still uncertain.

playbooks.name: Research and Reasoning
playbooks.description: Provides the evidence-first operating model for synthesis and challenge review.

playbooks.name: Source Grounded Research
playbooks.description: Keeps the answer anchored to stronger sources instead of loose summaries.

playbooks.name: Landscape Scan
playbooks.description: Builds comparative views when the user needs options, categories, or market context.

playbooks.name: Briefing Synthesis
playbooks.description: Turns scattered findings into a memo, brief, or decision-ready summary.
